text.title: Reflection, Tree-of-Thoughts, and Multi-Agent Patterns
text.summary: Reflection loop calibration, Tree-of-Thoughts pruning, multi-agent topologies, and model tiering across patterns.

section.title: Reflection

rule.statement: Provide a concrete pass threshold in the critic prompt (e.g., "≤1 minor defect = pass"); a critic prompted too harshly always finds defects and the loop never terminates.
rule.rationale: Without a concrete exit condition, Reflection loops run to MAX_CYCLES every time, consuming tokens without improvement after the first cycle.

rule.statement: Use a structured critique rubric with explicit criteria and per-criterion scores; "could be better" feedback does not produce actionable revisions.
rule.rationale: Vague critique causes the revision pass to produce cosmetic changes only. Explicit criteria (accuracy, completeness, tone, length) yield targeted improvements.

section.reference: Structured critic prompt returning JSON pass/fail with defect list

section.title: Tree-of-Thoughts

rule.statement: Define a cheap heuristic evaluator first; using another LLM call as branch evaluator doubles cost per branch.
rule.rationale: ToT without pruning causes state-space explosion at depth 3+. An LLM evaluator that costs as much as generation makes ToT 2-4x more expensive than standard generation with no quality gain for most tasks.

section.p: Use ToT when: creative ideation, puzzle-like problems, strategic planning, or when a single reasoning path reliably fails. Cap tree depth at 3 for practical use.

section.title: Multi-Agent Topologies
section.p: Sequential: linear chain; low complexity. Parallel: concurrent execution; medium complexity. Supervisor/Coordinator: routing agent dispatches to specialists; medium complexity. Hierarchical: multi-level decomposition; high complexity. Swarm: all-to-all; highest complexity.

rule.statement: Each worker in a Supervisor pattern must have a well-defined scope and return structured output; open-ended worker responses cause the supervisor to misroute or re-assign the same task.
rule.rationale: Supervisor patterns fail silently when worker output is ambiguous. The supervisor interprets unstructured output as incomplete work and re-dispatches indefinitely.

rule.statement: For Supervisor patterns managing external actions (API calls, emails, database writes), add a human approval step before the supervisor dispatches to workers.
rule.rationale: Prevents cascading errors from misrouted tasks. One wrong dispatch can trigger irreversible side effects across multiple workers.

section.title: Pattern Selection

rule.statement: Choose the simplest pattern that solves the problem: single ReAct agent before Plan-Execute, single-agent before multi-agent.
rule.rationale: Each pattern layer adds latency, cost, and failure modes. Complexity should be justified by a clear limitation of the simpler approach.

section.p: Decision path: simple single-step → direct prompt. External data/actions → Tool Use. Dynamic adaptive workflow → ReAct. Multi-phase with dependencies → Plan-Execute. Quality-critical → add Reflection. Multiple expertise areas → Multi-Agent.
